title: Letters from a Stoic
author: Seneca
note: Timeless advice on how to live. Every letter feels like a conversation with a wise friend.

title: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
quote: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
note: A career handbook for developers. Covers everything from personal responsibility to pragmatic approaches for building better software.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: The definitive guide to distributed systems. Makes complex topics like replication, partitioning, and consensus feel intuitive.

title: Lex Fridman Podcast
author: Lex Fridman
note: Deep conversations about AI, science, and philosophy.

title: Men believe not what is true, but what is convenient
author: YouTube

title: Anything you lose by being real is fake
author: YouTube

title: If you show up every day, the results don't have a choice
author: YouTube

title: Only a slave quantifies its existence through productivity
author: YouTube

title: Do Things That Don't Scale
author: Paul Graham
note: Why startups must do unscalable things early — recruit users manually, give insane service, build by hand. The founders make it take off, not the product alone.

title: How To Be Successful
author: Sam Altman
note: 13 principles for outlier success: compound yourself, have almost too much self-belief, think independently, get good at sales, and make it easy to take risks.

title: How to Make Wealth
author: Paul Graham
note: Wealth is created, not divided. Startups compress a working life into a few years — you trade comfort for intensity and leverage technology to multiply output.

title: The Anatomy of Determination
author: Paul Graham
note: Determination beats intelligence as the strongest predictor of startup success. It's willfulness plus discipline plus ambition plus resilience.

title: You have power over your mind - not outside events.
quote: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
note: The personal journal of a Roman emperor. Raw, honest self-examination.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
note: Principles and patterns for writing readable, maintainable code. Changed how I think about naming, functions, and craftsmanship.

title: Neuroplasticity
author: Vida Demarin & Sandra Morović
note: The brain's ability to change and reorganize throughout life. Neural networks form and disappear based on experience — use it or lose it.

title: The only way to go fast, is to go well.
quote: The only way to go fast, is to go well.
author: Robert C. Martin

title: The Art of Computer Programming
author: Donald Knuth
note: The magnum opus of computer science. Dense, rigorous, and endlessly rewarding for anyone serious about algorithms.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
note: The original adventure story. Odysseus' ten-year journey home is a meditation on perseverance, identity, and what it means to belong.

title: Sustainable solutions based on innovation can create a more resilient world only if that innovation is focused on the health and well-being of its inhabitants.
quote: Sustainable solutions based on innovation can create a more resilient world only if that innovation is focused on the health and well-being of its inhabitants. And it is at that point — where technology and human needs intersect — that we will find meaningful innovation.
author: Frans van Houten

title: The number one benefit of information technology is that it empowers people to do what they want to do.
quote: The number one benefit of information technology is that it empowers people to do what they want to do. It lets people be creative. It lets people be productive. It lets people learn things they didn't think they could learn before, and so in a sense it is all about potential.
author: Steve Ballmer

title: I was laid off by Atlassian
author: Vasilios Syrakis
note: In this video, software engineer **Vasilios Syrakis** reflects on his 8-year tenure at [Atlassian](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=55pTFVoclvE) following a recent layoff. He details his technical contributions, most notably building an internal "Open Service Broker" and developing "[Sovereign](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=55pTFVoclvE)," a dynamic control plane for **Envoy Proxy** that automated infrastructure provisioning via templates. Beyond the technical architecture—which included leveraging AWS, HashiCorp Packer, and a sidecar model for microservices—Syrakis discusses the professional "soft" skills he cultivated, emphasizing that while building software is straightforward, the true long-term challenge lies in maintenance, team dynamics, conflict resolution, and the complexities of effective mentoring.
